subject: Physics
title: LIGO Detects Gravitational Waves
description: One-minute skill: LIGO Detects Gravitational Waves.

hook.text: LIGO detected a wobble in spacetime smaller than one-thousandth the width of a proton. It was gravitational waves.

buildup.text: Two L-shaped detectors, 3,000 km apart, each fire lasers down 4 km arms and measure length changes.

discovery.text: On September 14, 2015, both detectors registered the same chirp signal—from merging black holes 1.3 billion light-years away.

twist.text: The detection happened during an engineering test run. Scientists initially thought it might be a drill.

climax.text: It confirmed Einstein's 100-year-old prediction and opened a new way to observe the universe.

punchline.text: A wiggle smaller than a proton. Heard across a billion light-years. New astronomy.

quiz.question: How sensitive is LIGO's measurement?
options: It detects length changes smaller than one-thousandth the width of a proton
It measures changes of about one millimeter
It detects temperature shifts of one degree
